things.name: Marienplatz & Glockenspiel
things.description: Munich's central square with the New Town Hall's famous Glockenspiel carillon show daily at 11am

things.name: Deutsches Museum
things.description: The world's largest science and technology museum on a Isar island — 73,000 exhibits across 80 departments

things.name: English Garden (Englischer Garten)
things.description: One of the world's largest urban parks — bigger than Central Park, with the famous river surfers

things.name: Nymphenburg Palace
things.description: Magnificent Baroque summer palace with formal gardens, carriage museum and the Porcelain Museum

things.name: Oktoberfest at Theresienwiese
things.description: The world's largest folk festival with 14 giant beer tents — held annually in late September

things.name: Alte Pinakothek
things.description: World-class collection of European Old Masters from Raphael, Rubens and Dürer in a grand 1836 building

things.name: BMW World & Museum
things.description: Spectacular futuristic showroom and museum celebrating BMW's history alongside the Olympic Park

things.name: Dachau Concentration Camp Memorial
things.description: The first Nazi concentration camp, now a sobering memorial and educational site northwest of Munich

things.name: Viktualienmarkt
things.description: Munich's famous daily food market with Bavarian specialities, beer garden and flower stalls

things.name: Neuschwanstein Castle Day Trip
things.description: The fairy-tale castle that inspired Disney — 2 hours from Munich by train in the Bavarian Alps
